title: Choose Interview Clothes
summary: Select clean, appropriate, and professional clothing that fits the type of job you are interviewing for.
learnerPromise: You will be able to choose an outfit that looks professional and feels comfortable, so you can focus on the interview instead of worrying about your clothes.
whyItMatters: Your appearance is part of your first impression. Clean, appropriate clothing signals respect for the interviewer and the opportunity.
realLifeUses: Deciding what to wear for a first interview at a coffee shop or retail store
Choosing an outfit for an office or front-desk position interview
Finding something presentable when you do not own formal clothes
Preparing a backup outfit in case of spills or weather issues on interview day
materialsNeeded: Clean clothes that fit you well
result: A complete, clean, and appropriate interview outfit laid out and ready to wear.
youWillLearn: How to match your outfit to the type of workplace you are interviewing at
How to check that your clothes are clean, wrinkle-free, and fit well
How to keep accessories and scents minimal and professional
How to plan your outfit in advance to avoid last-minute stress
miniChallenge: Look at what you own and lay out one complete interview outfit. Check that it is clean, fits well, and is appropriate for a specific job you might apply for.
steps: Research the workplace. A bank or office usually expects more formal clothing than a warehouse or casual restaurant.
Choose clean, wrinkle-free clothes in solid, neutral colors if possible. Avoid clothing with large logos or distracting graphics.
Make sure everything fits comfortably. You should be able to sit, stand, and move without adjusting your clothes constantly.
Keep accessories, makeup, and scents minimal. The goal is to look neat, not to make a fashion statement.
Lay out your full outfit the night before, including shoes and any needed underlayers, so you are not rushing.
completionCriteria: You have a complete outfit laid out that is clean, wrinkle-free, and fits the type of workplace
You can explain why this outfit is appropriate for the specific job you are considering
commonProblems: Wearing brand-new shoes that hurt — break in shoes beforehand or choose comfortable ones you know
Overdressing or underdressing for the workplace — when unsure, slightly more formal is usually safer
Wearing strong perfume or cologne — many workplaces are scent-free, and heavy scents can be distracting
Waiting until the last minute and discovering your clothes need washing or mending
tips: If you do not own professional clothes, thrift stores and clothing swaps are affordable options
Try on the full outfit and sit down in it — some clothes feel different when you are seated
Bring a small stain-removal pen or ask someone to check you for lint or pet hair before you go in
Personal grooming matters too — clean hair, trimmed nails, and fresh breath all support a polished appearance
